name: Gabon

colors.symbolism: the dense forests that cover much of the country and the timber industry
the equator, which runs through Gabon, and the sun
the South Atlantic Ocean along the country's west coast

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — green at the top, yellow in the middle and blue at the bottom. Aspect ratio 3:4.
history: Adopted on 9 August 1960, eight days before Gabon's independence from France on 17 August 1960. It replaced an earlier 1959–1960 flag that had carried the French tricolour in the canton; in the new flag the canton was removed and the central yellow stripe was widened to equal the others. The unusual 3:4 proportions are inherited from the colonial-era flag.

features.symbolism: green for the country's forests and natural resources, yellow for the equator that crosses Gabon and the sun, blue for the sea (the Atlantic coast)
